title: Precordial Thump
content_markdown:
#### Indications
- To be performed within 15 seconds of a witnessed ventricular fibrillation (VF) or pulseless ventricular tachycardia (VT) cardiac arrest. 

#### Procedure

- Confirm cardiac arrest: 
- No pulse and unconscious.
- Analyse ECG rhythm.

- If possible, have a second person confirm that a VF or pulseless VT cardiac arrest has occurred.

- If confirmed: 
- Hold one clenched fist approximately 30cms above patient’s mid sternum.
- Bring fist down sharply so that medial (ulna) aspect of fist makes contact with patient’s chest.

- Reassess patient for a central pulse and check the patient’s rhythm.

#### Contraindications
- Recent sternotomy for coronary artery bypass graft, valve replacement, or recent significant chest trauma.
